target.name: QuantumScape
target.founder: Jagdeep Singh, Tim Holme, Fritz Prinz

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.0B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
QuantumScape, a public Climate & Energy company, commands a current valuation above $5B following $2B+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws from a peer universe of 10 same-sector companies at similar valuation tiers, comprising 1 public comparable (LanzaTech, ticker LNZA) and 8 private peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and Form Energy.

The peer-median method provides the primary triangulation approach. The median valuation across the 10-company peer set stands at $3.4B, with individual peer valuations ranging from $1.0B to $6.0B. Notable peers in the sample include TAE Technologies and Redwood Materials, each valued at $6.0B; Helion at $5.4B; Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B; and Form Energy at $3.5B. This method yields an implied valuation of $3.4B.

The triangulated valuation range is $2.9B to $3.9B, centered on a median of $3.4B. The current $5B+ valuation sits meaningfully above peer medians, suggesting either a market premium for QuantumScape's stage advancement as a public entity or a valuation disconnect relative to the peer cohort. Key uncertainties include the limited applicability of only one valuation method in this analysis and the dispersion within the peer set, where the range spans $1.0B to $6.0B. Additional revenue-based or comparable transaction metrics would strengthen conviction in any terminal valuation estimate.
